behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-delay math/state progression logic. A real failure is in the backoff calculation itself, not in I/O boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff calculator/state machine directly: feed consecutive failure counts (1..N), assert delays follow exponential growth and clamp at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Rate-limit detection plus wait computation is primarily classification/parsing logic over response data and headers. It can fail without any module wiring issues.
behaviors.test_description: Call the retry-decision function with representative responses (e.g., 429, quota error payload, Retry-After present/absent) and assert retry/not-retry plus computed delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at boundaries between retry orchestrator, timer/scheduler, and transport adapter. Unit tests of one function can miss request replay bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client + retry layer + fake transport + fake clock. First response is rate-limited, second succeeds. Advance clock by expected wait, assert transport was called twice with equivalent request and caller receives success payload.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: The typed error must emerge through the full retry path, including error mapping and propagation across modules. Pure unit mapping tests may miss wrapping/regression in call flow.
behaviors.test_description: Using wired retry client and stub transport that always returns rate-limit, run until max retries reached and assert rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` (type/fields), not generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Termination depends on interaction of retry counter, quota tracking, and scheduler progression. Infinite-loop failures are orchestration failures across components.
behaviors.test_description: Run full client with fake clock and always-rate-limited transport; assert call settles within bounded simulated time/attempt count and stops at max retries with terminal error.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: This is shared-state/concurrency behavior across caller-facing API and quota manager. Real failures are race/coordination issues at module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/quota manager; fire concurrent `call()` invocations. Use controlled transport/quota limits and assert aggregate allowed attempts reflect one shared bucket, not per-caller buckets.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering guarantees depend on queue implementation plus dispatcher timing under async contention. Single-function tests can miss reorder bugs in wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Queue several `call()` requests while send path is blocked/throttled, then release capacity stepwise; assert dispatch/start order matches submission order (FIFO) and no overtaking occurs.
